questions.question: Which plan is required to access the Content Search feature?

options.text: Pro Plan
options.explanation: The Content Search feature is specifically listed as available only to users on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Free Plan
options.explanation: The Content Search feature is specifically listed as available only to users on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: According to the article, this feature is only available to users on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Workspace Owner Plan
options.explanation: The article specifies the Enterprise Plan as the requirement, not a Workspace Owner specific plan.

questions.question: How can workspace owners export data from Content Search?

options.text: By downloading a PDF report from the browser
options.explanation: The article specifies that owners can export the data as a .csv file, not a PDF.

options.text: By using the 'Export' button in the top right corner
options.explanation: The article does not mention a top-right corner export button; the export function is specific to workspace owners accessing the data.

options.text: By clicking 'Export' in the Settings menu
options.explanation: While accessed via Settings, the specific action mentioned is exporting the data as a .csv file from the search results.

options.text: By exporting the page list as a .csv file
options.explanation: The article states: 'Workspace owners can also export this data as a .csv file.'

questions.question: What information is displayed next to each page in the Content Search list?

options.text: Page icon, cover image, and last edit date
options.explanation: The article lists specific details like location, access status, creator, creation date, last editor, and edit date.

options.text: Location (private/shared), creator, creation date, last editor, and edit date
options.explanation: Next to each page, you will see where it lives, who created it, who can access it, and who last edited it.

options.text: Page ID, tags, and shared with list
options.explanation: While Page ID is searchable, the article describes the visible list details as location, creator, and editor information.

options.text: Only the page title and page creator
options.explanation: The article lists much more information than just the title and creator, including access status and edit history.

questions.question: How is the 'Audience' column interpreted when a page has multiple access levels?

options.text: It lists the specific group the user belongs to
options.explanation: The audience column does not list the specific group membership but rather a summary of access.

options.text: It lists the least restrictive group
options.explanation: The article explicitly states it lists the 'most permissive group' to ensure visibility of the broadest access.

options.text: It lists the most permissive group
options.explanation: The note clarifies that the column lists the most permissive group; for example, if a page is shared externally, that overrides 'Shared internally'.

options.text: It lists the owner of the page
options.explanation: The audience column describes the group sharing the page, not necessarily the owner.

questions.question: What is the first step to change permissions for a restricted page?

options.text: Select the page title to open the sidebar
options.explanation: To change permissions, you must navigate to Settings -> Content search first, then select the menu options next to the page.

options.text: Go to Settings -> Content search
options.explanation: The steps outline: 1. Go to Settings → Content search. 2. Select ••• next to the page’s title.

options.text: Click the 'Share' button on the page itself
options.explanation: The article specifically instructs to go to Settings -> Content search first, not to the individual page.

options.text: Click 'Change permissions' immediately after searching
options.explanation: There is no direct 'Change permissions' button visible immediately; it requires the specific menu selection next to the page title.

questions.question: Which fields can you use to search for pages in Content Search?

options.text: Page ID, title, or content
options.explanation: The article states you can 'search for a page based on its ID, title, or content'.

options.text: Only the page ID
options.explanation: You can search by ID, title, or content, not just ID.

options.text: Only the creator's name
options.explanation: While you can filter by creator, the direct search function covers ID, title, and content.

options.text: Only the content inside the blocks
options.explanation: You can search by ID and title as well as content.

questions.question: What filter options are available to narrow down search results?

options.text: Font size and color
options.explanation: Content search filters relate to metadata like dates and creators, not visual styling.

options.text: Created date, creator, and more
options.explanation: The article mentions you can 'filter by created date, creator, and more'.

options.text: Page size and last edited date
options.explanation: While last edited date is visible in the list, it is not listed as a primary filter option in the text provided.

options.text: Location type and access level
options.explanation: While location is listed in the results, it is not explicitly listed as a filter category in the text.

questions.question: How does the Content Search feature handle multiple Enterprise workspaces?

options.text: It only searches the current workspace
options.explanation: The article states that if an organization has multiple Enterprise workspaces, you can view and manage content across all of them in one place.

options.text: It requires a separate login for each workspace
options.explanation: The feature allows viewing and managing across all Enterprise workspaces without mentioning separate logins.

options.text: It automatically merges all workspaces into one view
options.explanation: The article confirms you can view and manage content across all Enterprise workspaces in one place.

options.text: It disables access to other workspaces
options.explanation: The feature is designed to aggregate content from multiple workspaces, not disable access.

questions.question: What caution is provided regarding the use of the 'Change permissions' feature?

options.text: It may expose personal data of workspace members and guests
options.explanation: The article includes a note warning that using this feature may provide access to personal data and suggests consulting legal counsel.

options.text: It violates data privacy laws in the EU
options.explanation: The article suggests consulting legal counsel for advice regarding laws, but does not state it violates them by definition.

options.text: It cannot be used on pages shared externally
options.explanation: The feature is intended to help manage permissions, including potentially restricting pages shared externally.

options.text: It requires domain verification to function
options.explanation: Domain verification is not required to enable the feature, according to the note at the top of the article.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as information visible next to a page in the Content Search results?

options.text: Who can access the page (private, shared internally, externally, or web)
options.explanation: This is explicitly listed as visible information next to each page.

options.text: Page icon and cover image
options.explanation: The article lists location, access, creator, creation date, last editor, and edit date. It does not mention visual elements like icons or covers.

options.text: Page creator and creation date
options.explanation: This is explicitly listed as visible information next to each page.

options.text: Who last edited the page and when
options.explanation: This is explicitly listed as visible information next to each page.
